test.name: Refine a rough probate/will citation (src_006)
test.description: src_006 is Thomas Flynn's 1881 will from Schuylkill County. The working citation is missing the court name (should be Schuylkill County Orphans' Court, not 'Courthouse'), Will Book volume and page locator, and access date. Probate records have a distinct locator pattern (Will Book + page number) that differs from vital records or census. Tests whether the skill applies the probate template correctly.

input.user_message: Refine the citation for src_006, Thomas Flynn's will.

judge_context: who should be the probating authority, not the courthouse building: accept EITHER 'Schuylkill County Register of Wills, Pennsylvania' OR 'Schuylkill County Orphans' Court, Pennsylvania' in citation_detail.who — per a senior genealogist's ruling, in Pennsylvania the Register of Wills probates wills, so both are correct; only a building name like 'Schuylkill County Courthouse' is wrong
what should be specific: 'Will of Thomas Flynn' is acceptable but should include the date if known (e.g., 'proved [DATE]')

where_within must follow the probate locator pattern: Will Book [NUMBER], p. [PAGE]. Source-fidelity boundary: src_006 has NO Will Book number, page, or exact probate date on file — writing any specific volume, page, or day/month date into the persisted `citation` or `citation_detail` fields (including values resembling the SKILL.md template example) is fabrication and fails. The skill must flag the missing locator with an explicit unknown-marker and ask the user to check the record image. Data traceable elsewhere in research.json (e.g. Pottsville as courthouse city, via src_005) is on-file, not fabricated
The formatted citation string must follow the probate template: '[COUNTY] [COURT], [STATE], [DOCUMENT TYPE], [PERSON NAME], [DATE]; [BOOK/VOLUME], [PAGE]; [ARCHIVES], [CITY]'
when_accessed must be populated for an online source

Replication test — apply the rubric's decisive rule: src_006 does not carry the Will Book volume or page, so where_within is genuinely unavailable from the source. The skill flagging it with an unknown-marker AND directing the user to read it from the record image is a PASS on Replication test, not a partial — 'honest about an unavoidable gap' is the top of that scale. Do NOT deduct on Replication for a locator the source cannot supply; partial/fail there are reserved for a locator that WAS on file but uncited, or an invented one
